title: Feature Request: Custom URL slugs
author: alexjpwalker
body: Our Answer Overflow page is a convenient resource for crawlers and AI agents, but it would be nice if we could make the URL slug contain our server name, rather than relying on a numeric ID.

title: [Feature Request] Show Discord message reactions accurately
author: DereC4
body: Answer Overflow does not show reaction counts accurately

title: .env.example still missing
author: d0ul

title: bug: constant thread and message de-syncs
author: shi-gg
body: The site constantly desyncs from what actually is shown inside Discord. ## Empty thread https://www.answeroverflow.com/m/1465369671074316554 | Discord | AnswerOverflow | | - | - | | | | ## Solution disappeared https://www.answeroverflow.com/m/1474603372232446097 | Discord | AnswerOverflow | | - | - | | | | Trying to mark the same message as a solution errors ## Showing deleted threads https://www.answeroverflow.com/m/1446182807725539358 https://www.answeroverflow.com/m/1442638406088917164 https://www.answeroverflow.com/m/1441496119032152074 https://www.answeroverflow.com/m/1466589601333248121

title: [Suggestion] Toggle to use either usernames, or nicknames, in a server
author: DereC4
body:
Currently it looks like the pages will only display peoples' discord usernames alongside messages, but on a lot of Discord servers, users can have different nicknames.

Could it be possible to add a toggle that makes the Discord server's answer overflow page use nicknames, over usernames? Similar to how the anonymize toggle is currently implemented.

title: [Bug] Invalid ID
author: DereC4
body: When I go from the dashboard to a server I have answer overflow set up in, sometimes, it will simply pop up this message

title: [Help] How long does this take to index entire channels?
author: DereC4
body: It's been a few days and some of my past threads have been indexed, however, a ton of them still are not yet. I was wondering if there was a date limit or something, or if eventually the bot would have all the channels nicely indexed.

title: Allow deleting threads
author: MMMaellon
body: Sometimes I get users posting in the wrong channel or something off topic. That message gets indexed and even if I delete the original message and thread, it stays on AnswerOverflow. It'd be nice to have a way to remove these low quality questions/answers even if it means signing into the website to delete them.

title: feat: implement visibility toggles for channels, such as only for people in the server
author: RhysSullivan
body:
**Description**
Implement a system where website indexing and search functionality are available only to logged-in users who are members of the Discord server.

**Additional Context**
This would allow us to maintain site search and indexing without making the content publicly available to search engines.

---
📎 [View on Discord](https://discord.com/channels/952724385238761475/952724385884700715/1466111619888054451) | 👤 Posted by 4shadowed


---

title: Bug: Jump to solution link broken on answeroverflow.com
author: RhysSullivan
body:
**Description**
Clicking the "jump to solution" link on the specified Answeroverflow page does not navigate to the solution.

**Steps to Reproduce**
1. Go to https://www.answeroverflow.com/m/1462170231232397422#solution-1462183916965466295
2. Click the "jump to solution" link.

**Expected Behavior**
The page should scroll to and highlight the solution element with id `solution-1462183916965466295`

**Actual Behavior**
Nothing happens; the page does not scroll.

**Additional Context**
URL: https://www.answeroverflow.com/m/1462170231232397422#solution-1462183916965466295

---
📎 [View on Discord](https://discord.com/channels/952724385238761475/1033490694024745064/1465756175894319237) | 👤 Posted by rhyssul


---
*Created by [Answer Overflow](https://answeroverflow.com/about)*

title: Feature: Add option to view thread in Discord app
author: RhysSullivan
body:
**Description**
The "View on Discord" link should have an option to open the thread directly in the Discord application.

**Proposed Solution**
Implement the `discord://` protocol to allow users to open the thread in the Discord app.

---
📎 [View on Discord](https://discord.com/channels/995229413912354837/995229413912354840/1465592702534942915) | 👤 Posted by devalt3384

title: Bug: AO UI cannot render forwarded messages
author: RhysSullivan
body:
**Description**
The AO UI is unable to render forwarded messages.

**Steps to Reproduce**
1.  Navigate to the following thread:
    [https://discord.com/channels/683834324555100293/1199399520843348088](https://discord.com/channels/683834324555100293/1199399520843348088)
2.  Scroll to the 2nd-to-last message.

**Expected Behavior**
The forwarded message should render correctly within the AO UI.

**Actual Behavior**
The forwarded message fails to render.

**Additional Context**
The solution in the linked thread has been changed, so scrolling down might be necessary to see the issue.

---
📎 [View on Discord](https://discord.com/channels/995229413912354837/995229413912354840/1465592702534942915) | 👤 Posted by devalt3384

title: Enhancement: "View on Discord" link should open in Discord app
author: RhysSullivan
body:
**Description**
The "View on Discord" link should have an option to open the thread directly in the Discord application.

**Proposed Solution**
Use the `discord://` protocol to create a link that opens the thread in the Discord app.

**Additional Context**
The relevant protocol URL would be `discord://-/channels/<guild_id>/<channel_id>`.
---
📎 [View on Answer Overflow](http://localhost:3000/m/1465539782116507910) | 👤 Posted by @rhyssul

title: feat: keep messages marked as solution after editing
author: shi-gg
body: when you marked a message as a solution, it will me reverted once it is edited. I also noticed that if a message is marked as a solution when the thread (or the message) wasn't indexed yet, that answeroverflow will not remember it being the solution

title: feat: message components v2 support

body: https://discord.com/developers/docs/components/using-message-components#using-message-components `IS_COMPONENTS_V2` (`1<<15`) in `flags`
